text.title: Secrets Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: GitHub repo contains AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID + AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY in repo secrets; rotation manual.
antipattern.reason: Team set up CI/CD before OIDC trust was available; static keys grandfathered in.
antipattern.note: Fix: Configure OIDC trust between GitHub and AWS IAM. Replace static keys with role assumption via id-token. Delete the IAM access keys.

antipattern.description: Production cluster has 50+ Secrets created via kubectl create secret; no rotation; manifests in private git.
antipattern.reason: ESO not installed; team uses imperative kubectl.
antipattern.note: Fix: Install ESO + the backend ClusterSecretStore. Replace plaintext Secrets with ExternalSecret manifests. Delete the plaintext Secrets after migration.

antipattern.description: Application can read every secret in the backend; security review flags it.
antipattern.reason: Initial policy used 'secrets/*' to unblock the team.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace with explicit paths: prod/api/* for the api role, prod/worker/* for the worker role. Audit logs surface the actual paths used.

antipattern.description: Audit shows a database password not rotated in 18 months; engineer who knew the rotation procedure left.
antipattern.reason: Rotation never automated; documented as a quarterly manual task that nobody owned.
antipattern.note: Fix: Configure backend's dynamic-credentials engine for the database. Rotation becomes automatic on lease expiry.

antipattern.description: Old image layer contains hardcoded API key; image still pullable from registry.
antipattern.reason: Dockerfile had ENV API_KEY=... during a hotfix.
antipattern.note: Fix: Remove ENV. Mount via secret volume or fetch at runtime via SDK. Rotate the leaked key. Audit registry for other contaminated images.
